points.text: Novelty Nobility advanced NN4101, a first-in-class bispecific antibody combining anti-c-Kit mAb and VEGF trap, into GMP manufacturing at AGC Biologics' Chiba facility for neovascular retinal diseases.

Medicenna Therapeutics presented preclinical data for MDNA113, a first-in-class anti-PD-1 x IL-2 bifunctional Superkine, demonstrating superior tolerability compared to anti-PD-1 x IL-2α-biased bispecifics in non-human primates.

MDNA113 was well tolerated at doses up to 50 mg/kg in non-human primates, whereas a comparator anti-PD-1 x IL-2α-biased bispecific induced severe toxicity including vascular leak syndrome at a fraction of that exposure.
Crescent Biopharma advanced its lead program CR-001, a PD-1 x VEGF bispecific antibody, in the ASCEND Phase 1/2 global trial for multiple solid tumor types.

BeOne Medicines received FDA priority review for tislelizumab plus zanidatamab (HER2-targeted bispecific antibody) and chemotherapy for first-line treatment of unresectable, locally advanced, or metastatic HER2-positive gastric/GEJ/esophageal adenocarcinoma.

The HERIZON-GEA-01 Phase 3 trial demonstrated that the tislelizumab/zanidatamab bispecific combination achieved median PFS of 12.4 months vs 8.1 months (HR 0.63, p<0.0001) and median OS of 26.4 months vs 19.2 months (HR 0.72, p=0.0043) in HER2-positive gastric cancer.
FDA granted breakthrough therapy designation to zanidatamab plus chemotherapy with or without tislelizumab for HER2-positive gastric cancer, validating the bispecific antibody's clinical utility.

Novelty Nobility — NN4101 — Neovascular retinal diseases — Expanded partnership with AGC Biologics for process development and GMP manufacturing at Chiba facility following successful cell line development in Copenhagen; first-in-class bispecific antibody (anti-c-Kit mAb + VEGF trap).

Medicenna Therapeutics — MDNA113 — Cancer immunotherapy — First-in-class anti-PD-1 x IL-2 bifunctional Superkine; IND submission expected H2 2026 following positive non-human primate data showing 50 mg/kg tolerability vs severe toxicity in comparator bispecific.
Crescent Biopharma — CR-001 — Multiple Solid Tumors — ASCEND Phase 1/2 global trial ongoing evaluating PD-1 x VEGF bispecific antibody in first-line and previously treated patients.

BeOne Medicines — Tislelizumab/Zanidatamab — HER2-positive gastric/GEJ/esophageal adenocarcinoma — FDA granted priority review for sBLA; Phase 3 HERIZON-GEA-01 met primary endpoints with PFS HR 0.63 and OS HR 0.72.

Novelty Nobility — Bispecific antibody commercialization — NN4101 — Clinical-stage biotech advancing first-in-class bispecific (anti-c-Kit mAb + VEGF trap) for neovascular retinal diseases; partnered with AGC Biologics for global manufacturing scale-up.

AGC Biologics — Bispecific manufacturing capacity expansion — NN4101 program — Executed second partnership with Novelty Nobility; leveraging Chiba and new Yokohama facilities with single-use bioreactor technology for complex protein GMP production.

Medicenna Therapeutics — Development of tumor-anchored conditionally activated bifunctional Superkines — MDNA113 (anti-PD-1 x IL-2) — Preclinical data presented at AACR 2026; IND submission planned for H2 2026.
Crescent Biopharma — Next-generation oncology therapies — CR-001 (PD-1 x VEGF bispecific) + ADC pipeline — Clinical-stage; ASCEND Phase 1/2 trial active for solid tumors.

BeOne Medicines — Regulatory advancement of HER2 bispecific combination — Zanidatamab (HER2-targeted bispecific) + Tislelizumab — Priority review granted for 1L gastric cancer; Breakthrough Therapy Designation secured.

NN4101 — Mechanism: First-in-class bispecific antibody fusing fully human anti-c-Kit monoclonal antibody with VEGF trap — Phase: Pre-clinical/Manufacturing readiness — Population: Neovascular retinal disease patients — Comparator: None stated — Status: Technical transfer to Chiba facility for GMP manufacturing initiated.

MDNA113 — Mechanism: Tumor-anchored, conditionally activated anti-PD-1 x IL-2 bifunctional Superkine (β-enhanced IL-2) — Phase: Preclinical — Population: Non-human primates — Comparator: Anti-PD-1 x IL-2α-biased bispecific — Results: Tolerated at 50 mg/kg with PK consistent with approved anti-PD-1; comparator failed at fraction of exposure due to vascular leak syndrome.

CR-001 — Mechanism: PD-1 x VEGF bispecific antibody — Phase: 1/2 (ASCEND) — Population: Multiple solid tumor types (first-line and previously treated) — Comparator: Not specified — Status: Ongoing global trial.

Zanidatamab — Mechanism: HER2-targeted bispecific antibody (binds two distinct epitopes on HER2) — Phase: 3 (HERIZON-GEA-01) — Population: Unresectable, locally advanced, or metastatic HER2-positive gastric/GEJ/esophageal adenocarcinoma — Comparator: Chemotherapy alone or with tislelizumab — Endpoints/Results: Median PFS 12.4 vs 8.1 months (HR 0.63, p<0.0001); Median OS 26.4 vs 19.2 months (HR 0.72, p=0.0043); Grade 3+ TR-TEAEs 71.8% in combination arm.

Bispecific antibodies (general class) — Mechanism: Dual-target engagement (CRS risk) — Phase: Clinical use — Population: Oncology patients — Comparator: Inpatient monitoring — Endpoints/Results: Outpatient monitoring kits (BP, O2, temp) and virtual visits demonstrated safety net efficacy comparable to inpatient care for CRS mitigation.

Novelty Nobility / AGC Biologics — Manufacturing partnership expansion — NN4101 bispecific program — Second collaboration agreement executed; AGC Biologics to handle process development and GMP manufacturing at Chiba facility following Copenhagen cell line development success.
